title: Vismia tenuinervia
intro: Vismia tenuinervia is a plant species commonly known as "Rakas" or "Vismia," which belongs to the family Clusiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Vismia tenuinervia include flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, as well as terpenoids and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Vismia tenuinervia is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in the form of extracts or capsules to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Vismia tenuinervia is a species of flowering plant in the family Clusiaceae, commonly known as the stovepipe tree. It is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. As a member of the Clusiaceae family, Vismia tenuinervia is closely related to other plants in this family, which are primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. The Clusiaceae family is part of the order Malpighiales, which also includes other plant families such as the Euphorbiaceae and the Salicaceae. Within the Clusiaceae family, Vismia tenuinervia is classified as a single species within the genus Vismia.
